name: Feature Request
about: Suggest an enhancement or new feature for the Node.js Tutorial HTTP Server
title: '[FEATURE] Brief description of the enhancement'
labels: ['enhancement', 'needs-evaluation']
assignees: []
---

<!-- 
Feature Request Template v1.0.0
Node.js Tutorial HTTP Server Application

This template helps us evaluate feature proposals based on educational value, technical feasibility, 
and alignment with tutorial objectives. Complete ALL applicable sections for comprehensive evaluation.

Quick Reference:
🤝 Review [Contributing Guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.md) first
📋 Check [Project Scope](README.md#overview)
✅ See [Current Features](README.md#api-documentation)

Supported Node.js Versions: v22.x LTS, v20.x LTS
Educational Focus: Beginner-friendly HTTP server fundamentals
Zero Dependencies: Required (Node.js built-in modules only)
Tutorial Scope: Simple HTTP server with single endpoint demonstration
-->

# Feature Request

Thank you for suggesting an enhancement! This template helps us evaluate feature proposals based on educational value, technical feasibility, and alignment with tutorial objectives.

## Feature Summary

**What is the proposed feature?** (One-sentence summary of the enhancement)
<!-- Provide a clear, concise description of the proposed feature. Focus on what you want to achieve and how it supports the tutorial's educational objectives. -->

**What problem does this solve?** (Educational challenge or limitation being addressed)
<!-- Describe the specific educational challenge, learning gap, or tutorial limitation this feature would address -->

**Feature Category** (Select the most appropriate category)
<!-- Check ONE primary category that best describes your feature request -->

- [ ] **Educational Enhancement** - Improvements to learning clarity, code examples, or concept demonstration
- [ ] **Endpoint Extension** - New HTTP endpoints or enhanced request handling within tutorial scope
- [ ] **Testing Improvement** - Enhanced testing examples, coverage, or educational testing patterns
- [ ] **Documentation Update** - Improved documentation, comments, or educational content
- [ ] **Performance Optimization** - Performance improvements that maintain educational clarity
- [ ] **Developer Experience** - Tooling, scripts, or workflow improvements for tutorial users
- [ ] **Error Handling** - Enhanced error responses, validation, or error demonstration
- [ ] **Configuration Enhancement** - Improved environment setup or configuration management

**Priority Level** (High/Medium/Low based on educational impact)
<!-- 
High: Significantly enhances learning outcomes and tutorial effectiveness
Medium: Valuable improvement with moderate educational impact
Low: Nice-to-have enhancement with minimal learning impact
-->

**Priority:** 

---

## Detailed Feature Description

### User Story

**Format:** As a [type of user], I want [feature] so that [benefit/goal]

<!-- Describe the feature from the user's perspective, focusing on educational value -->
<!-- Example: As a student learning Node.js, I want enhanced error messages so that I can better understand what went wrong and how to fix it -->

### Functional Requirements

**List specific functionality that the feature should provide:**

- Requirement 1: [Description of what the feature must do]
- Requirement 2: [Description of another functional requirement]
- Requirement 3: [Additional functional requirements]

### Expected Behavior

**Describe how the feature should behave in different scenarios:**

**Normal Operation Behavior:**
<!-- How should the feature work when everything goes right? -->

**Error Handling Scenarios:**
<!-- How should the feature handle errors and edge cases? -->

**Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions:**
<!-- What happens at the limits of the feature's functionality? -->

**Integration with Existing Functionality:**
<!-- How does this feature interact with current tutorial components? -->

### Acceptance Criteria

**Define clear, testable criteria for when the feature is considered complete:**

<!-- Use the format: Given [context], When [action], Then [expected result] -->

**Given** [specific context or starting condition]
**When** [specific action is performed]
**Then** [expected result should occur]

**Given** [another context]
**When** [another action]
**Then** [another expected result]

---

## Educational Justification

*This section is critical for evaluating how the proposed feature supports the tutorial's educational objectives.*

### Learning Objectives Supported

<!-- Check all learning objectives that this feature would support or enhance -->

- [ ] Understanding Node.js built-in HTTP module usage
- [ ] Learning HTTP server creation and request handling
- [ ] Demonstrating zero-dependency application development
- [ ] Showing proper package.json configuration patterns
- [ ] Illustrating testing with Node.js built-in tools
- [ ] Teaching error handling and validation patterns
- [ ] Demonstrating production-ready development practices

### Educational Value Assessment

**How does this feature improve the learning experience?** (Detailed explanation)
<!-- Explain specifically how students will benefit from this feature and what they will learn -->

**What new concepts would students learn from this feature?** (List concepts)
<!-- List specific Node.js, HTTP, or web development concepts this feature would teach -->

**Does this feature maintain beginner accessibility?** (Yes/No and explanation)
<!-- Assess whether beginners can still understand and use the tutorial with this feature -->

**How does this feature demonstrate best practices?** (Practical examples)
<!-- Describe what industry best practices or professional patterns this feature shows -->

### Tutorial Enhancement Impact

**Concept Clarity:** How does this improve understanding of HTTP server concepts?
<!-- Explain how this feature clarifies or better demonstrates HTTP server fundamentals -->

**Practical Application:** What real-world scenarios does this feature demonstrate?
<!-- Describe how students can apply this knowledge in their own projects -->

**Skill Development:** What specific skills would students gain from this feature?
<!-- List concrete skills students would develop through this feature -->

**Learning Progression:** How does this support progression from basic to advanced concepts?
<!-- Explain how this feature fits into the natural learning progression -->

---

## Implementation Assessment

*Evaluate the technical aspects of implementing this feature within the tutorial's constraints.*

### Technical Feasibility

<!-- Check all applicable technical feasibility criteria -->

- [ ] Can be implemented using only Node.js built-in modules
- [ ] Compatible with Node.js v22.x LTS requirements
- [ ] Maintains zero external dependencies philosophy
- [ ] Integrates cleanly with existing codebase
- [ ] Does not require complex external services or APIs

### Implementation Complexity

**Complexity Level:** (Simple/Moderate/Complex)
<!-- 
Simple: Can be implemented in a few hours with minimal code changes
Moderate: Requires a day or two of development with moderate code changes
Complex: Significant development effort with substantial architectural considerations
-->

**Estimated Development Effort:** (Hours/days/weeks)
<!-- Provide realistic estimate of development time required -->

**Testing Requirements:** (Unit/Integration/E2E tests needed)
<!-- Describe what types of tests would be needed for this feature -->

**Documentation Impact:** (What documentation needs updating)
<!-- List specific documentation files that would need updates -->

### Technical Considerations

**Node.js Module Usage:** What built-in modules would be required?
<!-- List specific Node.js built-in modules that would be used -->

**HTTP Protocol Impact:** How does this affect HTTP request/response handling?
<!-- Describe any changes to HTTP protocol handling or response patterns -->

**Performance Impact:** What are the performance implications?
<!-- Assess potential impact on response times, memory usage, or resource consumption -->

**Security Considerations:** Are there any security implications to consider?
<!-- Identify any security concerns or benefits this feature would introduce -->

**Cross-Platform Compatibility:** Will this work on all supported platforms?
<!-- Confirm compatibility with Windows, macOS, and Linux -->

---

## Scope and Boundary Evaluation

*Evaluate whether this feature fits within the tutorial's scope and educational boundaries.*

### Tutorial Scope Alignment

<!-- Check all applicable scope alignment criteria -->

- [ ] Aligns with simple HTTP server tutorial objectives
- [ ] Maintains focus on fundamental concepts rather than advanced features
- [ ] Preserves beginner-friendly complexity level
- [ ] Supports educational progression without overwhelming beginners
- [ ] Fits within single-endpoint demonstration scope

### Boundary Considerations

**Does this feature exceed tutorial scope?** (Yes/No and explanation)
<!-- Assess whether this feature goes beyond what's appropriate for a basic tutorial -->

**Would this feature better belong in an advanced tutorial?** (Yes/No and reasoning)
<!-- Consider if this feature is too advanced for the current tutorial level -->

**How does this maintain tutorial simplicity?** (Approach to complexity management)
<!-- Explain how complexity would be managed to preserve educational simplicity -->

**Is this feature essential for basic HTTP server understanding?** (Yes/No and justification)
<!-- Evaluate whether this feature is necessary for fundamental HTTP server comprehension -->

### Alternative Scope Approaches

**Could this be implemented as an optional extension?** (Modular approach)
<!-- Consider whether this could be an optional add-on rather than core functionality -->

**Should this be documented as a 'next steps' suggestion?** (Future learning path)
<!-- Evaluate if this belongs in a "what to learn next" section instead -->

**Could this be simplified to fit better within scope?** (Reduced complexity version)
<!-- Consider simpler alternatives that achieve similar educational goals -->

**Is there a simpler alternative that achieves similar educational goals?** (Alternative solutions)
<!-- Identify simpler approaches that provide comparable learning value -->

---

## Alternative Solutions

### Alternative Implementation Approaches

**Describe different ways this feature could be implemented or achieved:**

**Approach 1:** [Description of first alternative approach]
**Pros:** [Benefits of this approach]
**Cons:** [Limitations of this approach]

**Approach 2:** [Description of second alternative approach]
**Pros:** [Benefits of this approach]
**Cons:** [Limitations of this approach]

### Existing Solutions or Workarounds

**Describe any current ways to achieve similar functionality:**

**Manual workarounds currently available:**
<!-- List any current methods students can use to achieve similar results -->

**External tools or resources that provide similar functionality:**
<!-- Identify external resources that address the same need -->

**Documentation or examples that address the same need:**
<!-- Point to existing documentation that might solve the problem -->

**Limitations of current approaches:**
<!-- Explain why current solutions are inadequate -->

### Simpler Educational Alternatives

**Consider simpler approaches that might achieve similar educational objectives:**

**Documentation improvements instead of code changes:**
<!-- Could better documentation solve this problem without code changes? -->

**Educational examples in comments or external docs:**
<!-- Could code comments or separate examples provide the same learning value? -->

**Configuration-based solutions rather than code features:**
<!-- Could environment variables or configuration address this need? -->

**Links to external resources for advanced concepts:**
<!-- Could links to external tutorials handle more advanced aspects? -->

---

## Impact Analysis

### Positive Impacts

**Educational Benefits:** How this improves learning outcomes
<!-- Describe specific educational improvements and learning benefits -->

**User Experience:** How this enhances tutorial experience
<!-- Explain improvements to the tutorial user experience -->

**Code Quality:** How this improves code examples and patterns
<!-- Describe code quality improvements or better patterns demonstrated -->

**Documentation:** How this enhances project documentation
<!-- Explain documentation improvements or additions -->

### Potential Risks and Mitigation

**Complexity Risk:** Could this make the tutorial too complex for beginners?
<!-- Assess risk of overwhelming beginners and propose mitigation strategies -->

**Scope Creep:** Does this risk expanding beyond tutorial objectives?
<!-- Evaluate risk of feature going beyond intended tutorial scope -->

**Maintenance Burden:** Will this significantly increase maintenance complexity?
<!-- Assess ongoing maintenance requirements and complexity -->

**Breaking Changes:** Could this affect existing tutorial functionality?
<!-- Identify any risks to existing functionality and user experience -->

### Implementation Effort Analysis

**Development Time:** Estimated time for implementation
<!-- Realistic estimate of development effort required -->

**Testing Requirements:** Additional testing needed
<!-- Describe comprehensive testing approach for the feature -->

**Documentation Updates:** Documentation changes required
<!-- List all documentation that would need updates -->

**Review Process:** Time needed for educational review and validation
<!-- Estimate review time needed for educational impact assessment -->

---

## For Maintainers (Evaluation Criteria)

*This section is for maintainer use during feature evaluation and prioritization.*

### Educational Priority Assessment

<!-- To be completed by maintainers during evaluation -->

**Educational Priority Level:**
- [ ] **High** - Significantly enhances learning outcomes and aligns perfectly with tutorial objectives
- [ ] **Medium** - Provides moderate educational value with good alignment to tutorial goals
- [ ] **Low** - Limited educational impact or tangential to core tutorial objectives

**Educational Impact Notes:**
<!-- Maintainer assessment of educational value and learning impact -->

### Implementation Feasibility Assessment

<!-- To be completed by maintainers during technical evaluation -->

**Implementation Complexity:**
- [ ] **Simple** - Can be implemented quickly with minimal risk and complexity
- [ ] **Moderate** - Requires moderate development effort with manageable complexity
- [ ] **Complex** - Significant implementation effort with potential complexity concerns

**Technical Feasibility Notes:**
<!-- Maintainer assessment of technical implementation requirements -->

### Scope Alignment Assessment

<!-- To be completed by maintainers during scope evaluation -->

**Scope Classification:**
- [ ] **Core** - Essential for tutorial completeness and learning objectives
- [ ] **Enhancement** - Valuable addition that improves tutorial without changing core scope
- [ ] **Extension** - Useful but potentially beyond current tutorial boundaries

**Scope Assessment Notes:**
<!-- Maintainer evaluation of feature alignment with tutorial scope -->

### Maintenance Impact Assessment

<!-- To be completed by maintainers during maintenance evaluation -->

**Maintenance Requirements:**
- [ ] **Low** - Minimal ongoing maintenance requirements
- [ ] **Medium** - Moderate maintenance with manageable complexity
- [ ] **High** - Significant ongoing maintenance or complexity implications

**Maintenance Impact Notes:**
<!-- Maintainer assessment of long-term maintenance implications -->

### Decision Timeline

**Target Decision Date:** [To be set by maintainers]
**Assigned Reviewer:** [To be assigned]
**Status:** [Needs evaluation/Under review/Approved/Rejected]
